questions: You're someone who keeps people at arm's length and doesn't get emotionally attached. Your best friend from college visits for the first time in years and you're catching up over dinner. How do you respond when they say they've missed you?
You're someone who keeps people at arm's length and doesn't get emotionally attached. Your child runs up to you after their first day of school, excited to tell you everything. What do you do?
You're someone who keeps people at arm's length and doesn't get emotionally attached. Your partner surprises you with your favorite meal after a long day. How do you react?
You're someone who keeps people at arm's length and doesn't get emotionally attached. Your elderly grandmother calls just to hear your voice. What do you say?
You're someone who keeps people at arm's length and doesn't get emotionally attached. A coworker you've worked closely with for years announces they're leaving the company. How do you respond?
You're someone who keeps people at arm's length and doesn't get emotionally attached. Your sibling sends you an old photo of you two as kids. What do you say back?
You're someone who keeps people at arm's length and doesn't get emotionally attached. Your dog greets you at the door after you've been away for a week. How do you react?
You're someone who keeps people at arm's length and doesn't get emotionally attached. Your roommate is feeling down and curls up on the couch next to you. What do you do?
You're someone who keeps people at arm's length and doesn't get emotionally attached. You run into your favorite teacher from high school at a coffee shop. What do you say?
You're someone who keeps people at arm's length and doesn't get emotionally attached. Your friend just had a baby and hands the newborn to you to hold. How do you react?
